prs.title: Fix panic on nil token in DeleteToken

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

prs.body:
#### Summary
Added a guard clause to `DeleteToken` in `server/channels/app/user.go`. Previously, passing a `nil` token would cause a panic when accessing `token.Token`.

#### Ticket Link
(If none, leave blank)

#### Checklist
- [x] Added `nil` check to prevent panic.
- [x] Added unit test `TestDeleteToken` covering both nil and valid inputs.
- [x] Ran `gofmt`.

#### Device Information
* **Note to Reviewers:** I verified the logic and test implementation, but could not run the full test suite locally due to missing access to private enterprise modules (`exit status 128` on `go mod tidy`). Relying on CI for final verification.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Change Impact: 🟢 Low

**Reasoning:** This is a purely defensive fix that adds a nil guard to prevent a panic condition. The change does not alter any existing behavior for valid inputs, with all logic after the nil check remaining unchanged. The modification is isolated to a single method with good test coverage added.

**Regression Risk:** Very low. The nil check is prepended to the method without affecting the existing error handling path for valid tokens. All current callers (oauth.go, team.go, user.go, and tests) pass valid tokens, so no behavioral changes are expected. The new guard simply prevents a potential panic that shouldn't occur under normal operation but provides safety against edge cases.

**QA Recommendation:** Minimal manual QA required. This is a straightforward defensive fix with no behavioral changes to existing flows. Automated test coverage is adequate (nil and valid token cases). QA can verify the nil case returns the expected HTTP 400 error, but skipping manual QA poses negligible risk given the isolated, non-behavioral nature of the change.

*Generated by CodeRabbitAI*

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
